heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 87–558, § 176 Stat. 246July 27, 1962
label: 791 to 808 Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 87–558, § 176 Stat. 246July 27, 1962
notes: Section 791, R.S. § 2418, related to issuance of certificates or land bounty warrants or, at option of Treasury, script for certain amounts for service in the War of 1812 or in any of Indian wars since 1790 and prior to , and in Mexican War.Mar. 3, 1850
Section 792, R.S. § 2419, related to entry into service after commencement of Mexican War.
Section 793, R.S. § 2420, related to militia, volunteers, and State troops in service between , and .June 18, 1812Mar. 22, 1852

Section 794, R.S. § 2421, prohibited benefits under sections 791 to 793 of this title if the person received, or was entitled to receive, any military land bounty under any Act passed prior to .Mar. 22, 1852
Section 795, R.S. § 2422, added any period of captivity to actual service in computing service, for purposes of sections 791 to 793 of this title.
Section 796, R.S. § 2424, related to rights of widows of persons entitled to benefits.
Section 797, R.S. § 2428, related to widows and children of persons entitled to benefits under .section 800 of this title
Section 798, R.S. § 2429, related to effect of subsequent marriage of widow.
Section 799, R.S. § 2430, related to age of minors within intent of .section 797 of this title
Section 800, R.S. § 2425, related to equalization of bounties at 160 acres.
Section 801, R.S. § 2426, enumerated classes of persons whose right was dependent on length of service.
Section 802, R.S. § 2427, enumerated classes of persons whose right was independent of length of service.
Section 803, R.S. § 2431; , , related to parol proof of military service.act July 3, 1930, ch. 863, § 246 Stat. 1016
Section 804, R.S. § 2432; , , related to admissibility of prior evidence of service for additional allowances.act July 3, 1930, ch. 863, § 246 Stat. 1016
Section 805, R.S. § 2433, related to allowance for travel time in computing length of service.
Section 806, R.S. § 2434, extended provisions of bounty land laws to Indians.
Section 807, R.S. § 2435; , , related to evidence of right of pension being admissible to show right to bounty.act July 3, 1930, ch. 863, § 246 Stat. 1016
Section 808, R.S. § 2438, denied deserters a right to land bounties.
